description: Curated Traits & Personality SNP panel for GenomeInsight (P3-62). PRS-primary module with weight sets for educational attainment (Okbay et al. 2022) and cognitive ability (Savage et al. 2018). Individual SNPs for neuroticism (CRHR1 rs1396862), extraversion (WSCD2 rs2164273), and risk tolerance (CADM2 rs993137). DRD4 rs747302 proxy for dopamine receptor variation with coverage caveat. All findings hard-capped at ★★☆☆ (Moderate Evidence). Associative language only — no directive claims.
module_disclaimer: Traits & Personality findings reflect statistical associations observed in large population studies. They do not predict individual outcomes. Behavioral traits are shaped by complex interactions between many genes, environment, culture, and personal experience. These results are for research and educational purposes only.

pathways.name: Cognitive Ability & Educational Attainment
pathways.description: Polygenic risk scores derived from large-scale GWAS meta-analyses of cognitive performance and years of education. These scores reflect statistical associations at the population level and do not predict individual intelligence or academic achievement.

pathways.name: Personality Dimensions (Big Five)
pathways.description: Individual SNPs associated with Big Five personality dimensions. GWAS effect sizes for personality traits are very small, and these variants collectively explain only a tiny fraction of personality variation.

CC.effect_summary: No risk allele at CRHR1 neuroticism locus. No association with increased neuroticism at this variant.

CT.effect_summary: One copy of the T allele. Associated with modestly higher neuroticism scores in GWAS meta-analyses. Effect size is very small (β ≈ 0.01 SD).

TC.effect_summary: One copy of the T allele. Associated with modestly higher neuroticism scores in GWAS meta-analyses. Effect size is very small (β ≈ 0.01 SD).

TT.effect_summary: Two copies of the T allele. Associated with modestly higher neuroticism scores. Note: individual SNP effects on personality are very small and do not determine personality.

snps.recommendation_text: CRHR1 region variants have been associated with neuroticism in large GWAS meta-analyses (Nagel et al. 2018, N>449,000). Individual SNP effects are extremely small and should not be interpreted as deterministic. Neuroticism is a normal personality dimension influenced by many genetic and environmental factors.

GG.effect_summary: No association with increased extraversion at this locus.

GA.effect_summary: One copy of the A allele. Associated with modestly higher extraversion scores in GWAS studies. Effect size is very small.

AG.effect_summary: One copy of the A allele. Associated with modestly higher extraversion scores in GWAS studies. Effect size is very small.

AA.effect_summary: Two copies of the A allele. Associated with modestly higher extraversion scores. Individual SNP effects on personality are very small and non-deterministic.

snps.recommendation_text: WSCD2 region variants have been associated with extraversion in large GWAS meta-analyses (Nagel et al. 2018). Extraversion is a complex personality dimension influenced by many genetic and environmental factors. Individual SNP effects are not predictive.

TT.effect_summary: No association with increased openness to experience at this locus.

TC.effect_summary: One copy of the C allele. Associated with modestly higher openness to experience scores in GWAS.

CT.effect_summary: One copy of the C allele. Associated with modestly higher openness to experience scores in GWAS.

CC.effect_summary: Two copies of the C allele. Associated with modestly higher openness. Individual SNP effects are very small.

snps.recommendation_text: CTNNA2 variants have been associated with openness to experience. Evidence is preliminary — effect sizes are very small and replication is limited.

TT.effect_summary: No association with increased agreeableness at this locus.

TC.effect_summary: One copy of the C allele. Weak association with higher agreeableness in exploratory GWAS analyses.

CT.effect_summary: One copy of the C allele. Weak association with higher agreeableness in exploratory GWAS analyses.

CC.effect_summary: Two copies of the C allele. Weak association with higher agreeableness. Evidence is preliminary.

snps.recommendation_text: Agreeableness GWAS findings have limited replication. Individual variants explain negligible personality variance. This result is for research interest only.

CC.effect_summary: No association with altered conscientiousness at this locus.

CT.effect_summary: One copy of the T allele. Weak association with conscientiousness in exploratory GWAS analyses.

TC.effect_summary: One copy of the T allele. Weak association with conscientiousness in exploratory GWAS analyses.

TT.effect_summary: Two copies of the T allele. Weak association with conscientiousness. Evidence is preliminary.

snps.recommendation_text: Conscientiousness GWAS findings have limited replication. Individual variants explain negligible personality variance. This result is for research interest only.

pathways.name: Behavioral Traits
pathways.description: Individual SNPs associated with behavioral phenotypes including risk tolerance and dopamine signaling. These associations are based on population-level GWAS data and do not predict individual behavior.

AA.effect_summary: No risk allele at CADM2 risk tolerance locus. No association with altered risk-taking behavior at this variant.

AC.effect_summary: One copy of the C allele. Associated with modestly higher self-reported willingness to take risks in GWAS of >1 million participants.

CA.effect_summary: One copy of the C allele. Associated with modestly higher self-reported willingness to take risks in GWAS of >1 million participants.

CC.effect_summary: Two copies of the C allele. Associated with modestly higher self-reported risk tolerance. Effect size is very small and not predictive of individual behavior.

snps.recommendation_text: CADM2 variants are among the most replicated risk tolerance GWAS hits (Karlsson Linnér et al. 2019, N>1 million). Risk tolerance is influenced by many genetic and environmental factors. This variant explains a tiny fraction of variation.

TT.effect_summary: No proxy signal for DRD4 7-repeat VNTR at this tag SNP. Standard dopamine D4 receptor signaling inferred.

TC.effect_summary: One copy of proxy allele. May tag the DRD4 exon III 7-repeat VNTR, associated with novelty seeking in some studies. This is a proxy SNP with limited predictive accuracy.

CT.effect_summary: One copy of proxy allele. May tag the DRD4 exon III 7-repeat VNTR, associated with novelty seeking in some studies. This is a proxy SNP with limited predictive accuracy.

CC.effect_summary: Two copies of proxy allele. May tag the DRD4 7-repeat VNTR. The VNTR itself has been inconsistently associated with novelty seeking and ADHD. Proxy accuracy varies by ancestry.

snps.recommendation_text: DRD4 exon III VNTR has been associated with novelty seeking and ADHD susceptibility in candidate gene studies, but effect sizes are small and replication is inconsistent. This is a tag SNP proxy — direct VNTR genotyping is not available on microarray platforms.
snps.coverage_note: rs747302 is a tag SNP proxy for the DRD4 exon III VNTR (7-repeat allele). Direct VNTR genotyping is not available on 23andMe/microarray platforms. Proxy accuracy (r²) varies by ancestry and is moderate at best (r² ≈ 0.3–0.5 in EUR). Results should be interpreted with caution.

cross_module.note: DRD4 VNTR has been associated with ADHD susceptibility. See Gene Health module for ADHD-related findings.

scoring_rules.evidence_cap_description: All Traits & Personality findings are hard-capped at ★★☆☆ (Moderate Evidence) per PRD §3.4b, regardless of the underlying evidence level of individual SNPs or PRS components.
scoring_rules.prs_primary_description: This module uses PRS as the primary analysis method, reusing the generic PRS engine (P3-14). Individual SNPs are secondary and provide trait-level context.
scoring_rules.research_use_only_description: All PRS outputs must include 'Research Use Only' labels. PRS findings are never displayed alongside monogenic ClinVar findings.
scoring_rules.associative_language_rule: All finding text must use associative language ('associated with', 'linked to') rather than directive claims ('causes', 'determines', 'predicts'). No deterministic phrasing.
scoring_rules.drd4_proxy_rule: DRD4 rs747302 is a tag SNP proxy for the exon III VNTR. Results must display a coverage caveat noting limited proxy accuracy (r² varies by ancestry).

DRD4_proxy.r_squared_note: Proxy accuracy varies by ancestry; moderate LD in European populations. Direct VNTR genotyping not available on microarray.
DRD4_proxy.insufficient_data_reason: Tag SNP proxy for structural variant (VNTR). Accuracy limited by LD decay and population-specific haplotype structure.

prs_ancestry_mismatch.description: Both PRS weight sets are derived from EUR populations. Ancestry mismatch warning is mandatory when user's inferred ancestry differs from EUR.

cross_module_links.note: DRD4 VNTR has been associated with ADHD susceptibility in candidate gene studies.
